So Manasseh slept with his fathers, and they buried him in his own house: and Amon his son reigned in his stead.
Amon [was] two and twenty years old when he began to reign, and reigned two years in Jerusalem.
But he did [that which was] evil in the sight of the LORD, as did Manasseh his father: for Amon sacrificed unto all the carved images which Manasseh his father had made, and served them;
And humbled not himself before the LORD, as Manasseh his father had humbled himself; but Amon trespassed more and more.
And his servants conspired against him, and slew him in his own house.
But the people of the land slew all them that had conspired against king Amon; and the people of the land made Josiah his son king in his stead.
Josiah [was] eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned in Jerusalem one and thirty years.
And he did [that which was] right in the sight of the LORD, and walked in the ways of David his father, and declined [neither] to the right hand, nor to the left.

For in the eighth year of his reign, while he was yet young, he began to seek after the God of David his father: and in the twelfth year he began to purge Judah and Jerusalem from the high places, and the groves, and the carved images, and the molten images.

And they brake down the altars of Baalim in his presence; and the images, that [were] on high above them, he cut down; and the groves, and the carved images, and the molten images, he brake in pieces, and made dust [of them,] and strowed [it] upon the graves of them that had sacrificed unto them.
And he burnt the bones of the priests upon their altars, and cleansed Judah and Jerusalem.
And [so did he] in the cities of Manasseh, and Ephraim, and Simeon, even unto Naphtali, with their mattocks round about.
And when he had broken down the altars and the groves, and had beaten the graven images into powder, and cut down all the idols throughout all the land of Israel, he returned to Jerusalem.

Now in the eighteenth year of his reign, when he had purged the land, and the house, he sent Shaphan the son of Azaliah, and Maaseiah the governor of the city, and Joah the son of Joahaz the recorder, to repair the house of the LORD his God.

And when they came to Hilkiah the high priest, they delivered the money that was brought into the house of God, which the Levites that kept the doors had gathered of the hand of Manasseh and Ephraim, and of all the remnant of Israel, and of all Judah and Benjamin; and they returned to Jerusalem.
And they put [it] in the hand of the workmen that had the oversight of the house of the LORD, and they gave it to the workmen that wrought in the house of the LORD, to repair and amend the house:
Even to the artificers and builders gave they [it,] to buy hewn stone, and timber for couplings, and to floor the houses which the kings of Judah had destroyed.

And the men did the work faithfully: and the overseers of them [were] Jahath and Obadiah, the Levites, of the sons of Merari; and Zechariah and Meshullam, of the sons of the Kohathites, to set [it] forward; and [other of] the Levites, all that could skill of instruments of musick.
Also [they were] over the bearers of burdens, and [were] overseers of all that wrought the work in any manner of service: and of the Levites [there were] scribes, and officers, and porters.
And when they brought out the money that was brought into the house of the LORD, Hilkiah the priest found a book of the law of the LORD [given] by Moses.
And Hilkiah answered and said to Shaphan the scribe, I have found the book of the law in the house of the LORD. And Hilkiah delivered the book to Shaphan.
And Shaphan carried the book to the king, and brought the king word back again, saying, All that was committed to thy servants, they do [it. ]
And they have gathered together the money that was found in the house of the LORD, and have delivered it into the hand of the overseers, and to the hand of the workmen.
Then Shaphan the scribe told the king, saying, Hilkiah the priest hath given me a book. And Shaphan read it before the king.
And it came to pass, when the king had heard the words of the law, that he rent his clothes.
And the king commanded Hilkiah, and Ahikam the son of Shaphan, and Abdon the son of Micah, and Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah a servant of the king’s, saying,

Go, enquire of the LORD for me, and for them that are left in Israel and in Judah, concerning the words of the book that is found: for great [is] the wrath of the LORD that is poured out upon us, because our fathers have not kept the word of the LORD, to do after all that is written in this book.

And Hilkiah, and [they] that the king [had appointed,] went to Huldah the prophetess, the wife of Shallum the son of Tikvath, the son of Hasrah, keeper of the wardrobe; (now she dwelt in Jerusalem in the college:) and they spake to her to that [effect. ]
And she answered them, Thus saith the LORD God of Israel, Tell ye the man that sent you to me,
Thus saith the LORD, Behold, I will bring evil upon this place, and upon the inhabitants thereof, [even] all the curses that are written in the book which they have read before the king of Judah:

Because they have forsaken me, and have burned incense unto other gods, that they might provoke me to anger with all the works of their hands; therefore my wrath shall be poured out upon this place, and shall not be quenched.
And as for the king of Judah, who sent you to enquire of the LORD, so shall ye say unto him, Thus saith the LORD God of Israel [concerning] the words which thou hast heard;

Because thine heart was tender, and thou didst humble thyself before God, when thou heardest his words against this place, and against the inhabitants thereof, and humbledst thyself before me, and didst rend thy clothes, and weep before me; I have even heard [thee] also, saith the LORD.

Behold, I will gather thee to thy fathers, and thou shalt be gathered to thy grave in peace, neither shall thine eyes see all the evil that I will bring upon this place, and upon the inhabitants of the same. So they brought the king word again.
Then the king sent and gathered together all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.

And the king went up into the house of the LORD, and all the men of Judah, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and the priests, and the Levites, and all the people, great and small: and he read in their ears all the words of the book of the covenant that was found in the house of the LORD.

And the king stood in his place, and made a covenant before the LORD, to walk after the LORD, and to keep his commandments, and his testimonies, and his statutes, with all his heart, and with all his soul, to perform the words of the covenant which are written in this book.
And he caused all that were present in Jerusalem and Benjamin to stand [to it.] And the inhabitants of Jerusalem did according to the covenant of God, the God of their fathers.

And Josiah took away all the abominations out of all the countries that [pertained] to the children of Israel, and made all that were present in Israel to serve, [even] to serve the LORD their God. [And] all his days they departed not from following the LORD, the God of their fathers.
Moreover Josiah kept a passover unto the LORD in Jerusalem: and they killed the passover on the fourteenth [day] of the first month.
And he set the priests in their charges, and encouraged them to the service of the house of the LORD,

And said unto the Levites that taught all Israel, which were holy unto the LORD, Put the holy ark in the house which Solomon the son of David king of Israel did build; [it shall] not [be] a burden upon [your] shoulders: serve now the LORD your God, and his people Israel,
And prepare [yourselves] by the houses of your fathers, after your courses, according to the writing of David king of Israel, and according to the writing of Solomon his son.
And stand in the holy [place] according to the divisions of the families of the fathers of your brethren the people, and [after] the division of the families of the Levites.
So kill the passover, and sanctify yourselves, and prepare your brethren, that [they] may do according to the word of the LORD by the hand of Moses.

And Josiah gave to the people, of the flock, lambs and kids, all for the passover offerings, for all that were present, to the number of thirty thousand, and three thousand bullocks: these [were] of the king’s substance.

And his princes gave willingly unto the people, to the priests, and to the Levites: Hilkiah and Zechariah and Jehiel, rulers of the house of God, gave unto the priests for the passover offerings two thousand and six hundred [small cattle,] and three hundred oxen.

Conaniah also, and Shemaiah and Nethaneel, his brethren, and Hashabiah and Jeiel and Jozabad, chief of the Levites, gave unto the Levites for passover offerings five thousand [small cattle,] and five hundred oxen.
So the service was prepared, and the priests stood in their place, and the Levites in their courses, according to the king’s commandment.
And they killed the passover, and the priests sprinkled [the blood] from their hands, and the Levites flayed [them. ]

And they removed the burnt offerings, that they might give according to the divisions of the families of the people, to offer unto the LORD, as [it is] written in the book of Moses. And so [did they] with the oxen.
And they roasted the passover with fire according to the ordinance: but the [other] holy [offerings] sod they in pots, and in caldrons, and in pans, and divided [them] speedily among all the people.

And afterward they made ready for themselves, and for the priests: because the priests the sons of Aaron [were busied] in offering of burnt offerings and the fat until night; therefore the Levites prepared for themselves, and for the priests the sons of Aaron.

And the singers the sons of Asaph [were] in their place, according to the commandment of David, and Asaph, and Heman, and Jeduthun the king’s seer; and the porters [waited] at every gate; they might not depart from their service; for their brethren the Levites prepared for them.
So all the service of the LORD was prepared the same day, to keep the passover, and to offer burnt offerings upon the altar of the LORD, according to the commandment of king Josiah.
And the children of Israel that were present kept the passover at that time, and the feast of unleavened bread seven days.

And there was no passover like to that kept in Israel from the days of Samuel the prophet; neither did all the kings of Israel keep such a passover as Josiah kept, and the priests, and the Levites, and all Judah and Israel that were present, and the inhabitants of Jerusalem.
In the eighteenth year of the reign of Josiah was this passover kept.
After all this, when Josiah had prepared the temple, Necho king of Egypt came up to fight against Charchemish by Euphrates: and Josiah went out against him.

But he sent ambassadors to him, saying, What have I to do with thee, thou king of Judah? [I come] not against thee this day, but against the house wherewith I have war: for God commanded me to make haste: forbear thee from [meddling with] God, who [is] with me, that he destroy thee not.

Nevertheless Josiah would not turn his face from him, but disguised himself, that he might fight with him, and hearkened not unto the words of Necho from the mouth of God, and came to fight in the valley of Megiddo.
And the archers shot at king Josiah; and the king said to his servants, Have me away; for I am sore wounded.

His servants therefore took him out of that chariot, and put him in the second chariot that he had; and they brought him to Jerusalem, and he died, and was buried in [one of] the sepulchres of his fathers. And all Judah and Jerusalem mourned for Josiah.

And Jeremiah lamented for Josiah: and all the singing men and the singing women spake of Josiah in their lamentations to this day, and made them an ordinance in Israel: and, behold, they [are] written in the lamentations.
Now the rest of the acts of Josiah, and his goodness, according to [that which was] written in the law of the LORD,
And his deeds, first and last, behold, they [are] written in the book of the kings of Israel and Judah.
Then the people of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah, and made him king in his father’s stead in Jerusalem.
Jehoahaz [was] twenty and three years old when he began to reign, and he reigned three months in Jerusalem.
And the king of Egypt put him down at Jerusalem, and condemned the land in an hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold.
And the king of Egypt made Eliakim his brother king over Judah and Jerusalem, and turned his name to Jehoiakim. And Necho took Jehoahaz his brother, and carried him to Egypt.
Jehoiakim [was] twenty and five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem: and he did [that which was] evil in the sight of the LORD his God.
Against him came up Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, and bound him in fetters, to carry him to Babylon.
Nebuchadnezzar also carried of the vessels of the house of the LORD to Babylon, and put them in his temple at Babylon.

Now the rest of the acts of Jehoiakim, and his abominations which he did, and that which was found in him, behold, they [are] written in the book of the kings of Israel and Judah: and Jehoiachin his son reigned in his stead.
Jehoiachin [was] eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned three months and ten days in Jerusalem: and he did [that which was] evil in the sight of the LORD.
And when the year was expired, king Nebuchadnezzar sent, and brought him to Babylon, with the goodly vessels of the house of the LORD, and made Zedekiah his brother king over Judah and Jerusalem.
Zedekiah [was] one and twenty years old when he began to reign, and reigned eleven years in Jerusalem.
And he did [that which was] evil in the sight of the LORD his God, [and] humbled not himself before Jeremiah the prophet [speaking] from the mouth of the LORD.
And he also rebelled against king Nebuchadnezzar, who had made him swear by God: but he stiffened his neck, and hardened his heart from turning unto the LORD God of Israel.
Moreover all the chief of the priests, and the people, transgressed very much after all the abominations of the heathen; and polluted the house of the LORD which he had hallowed in Jerusalem.
And the LORD God of their fathers sent to them by his messengers, rising up betimes, and sending; because he had compassion on his people, and on his dwelling place:
But they mocked the messengers of God, and despised his words, and misused his prophets, until the wrath of the LORD arose against his people, till [there was] no remedy.

Therefore he brought upon them the king of the Chaldees, who slew their young men with the sword in the house of their sanctuary, and had no compassion upon young man or maiden, old man, or him that stooped for age: he gave [them] all into his hand.
And all the vessels of the house of God, great and small, and the treasures of the house of the LORD, and the treasures of the king, and of his princes; all [these] he brought to Babylon.
And they burnt the house of God, and brake down the wall of Jerusalem, and burnt all the palaces thereof with fire, and destroyed all the goodly vessels thereof.
And them that had escaped from the sword carried he away to Babylon; where they were servants to him and his sons until the reign of the kingdom of Persia:
To fulfil the word of the LORD by the mouth of Jeremiah, until the land had enjoyed her sabbaths: [for] as long as she lay desolate she kept sabbath, to fulfil threescore and ten years.

Now in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that the word of the LORD [spoken] by the mouth of Jeremiah might be accomplished, the LORD stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, that he made a proclamation throughout all his kingdom, and [put it] also in writing, saying,

Thus saith Cyrus king of Persia, All the kingdoms of the earth hath the LORD God of heaven given me; and he hath charged me to build him an house in Jerusalem, which [is] in Judah. Who [is there] among you of all his people? The LORD his God [be] with him, and let him go up. Ezra

textCards.body:
•「所愿建造的」（6节），原文是「所渴慕建造的」（英文ESV、NASB译本）。
「渴慕 pUD」原文在圣经中被译为男人对女人的「恋慕」（创三十四8；申二十一
11），又被译为人对神的「专心爱」（诗九十—14）。所罗门「恋慕」他「所渴慕建
造的」的城市，却渐渐远离了值得他「专心爱」的神（王上三3）。

textCards.body: 为耶和华的名建造殿字，见申12:5。上帝的"名"与某个地点联系起来，表示他在那里与他的子民同在，人可以在那里朝见上帝，向他祈求。然而，这不表示某个地方可以容纳或限制上帝：见2:6。作者的神学观念是，圣殿既表达上帝实际的临在，也表明他威荣的超越性。后来，"上帝在基督里成为肉身"的教义（见约2:21），就是出自这个观念。这里将宫室与圣殿相提并论，可能是要指出大卫之约的"家"与"国"之间有密切的联系（见代上17:14）。虽然作者多次提到所罗门的宫室（2:12,7:11,81.9:3-4,11），但没有提到宫室的建造过程（王上7:1-12）。

textCards.body: 所罗门征用境内的迦南人劳工来建造圣殿（见17-18节：参8:7-10注：代上22:1-4注）。在古代世界，主权国往往会征召臣服的民族来从事这样的工作。
textCards.searchText:

textCards.body: 幔子和门（4:22）把至圣所与圣所的其他部分隔开。幔子象征圣殿与摩西会幕一脉相承（出26:31-35）。希律圣殿的结构也与这里相似（太27:51）：基督死的时候，幔子从上到下裂为两半，表示作为"影儿"的摩西律法制度已经退出历史舞台，基督最后的献祭连同他献祭的果效已经完全显明（见来9:11-12.10:1）。

textCards.body: 圣殿为祭祀而设，也为祷告而设。对于敬拜，《旧约》经常把献祭（为赎罪、奉献或感恩）与祷告相提并论，作为敬拜者内心感情的表达。

textCards.body: 我…的子民，若是，上帝最大的心意，就是赦免痛悔的百姓，并医治他们的地。本节所用的词汇（自卑、祷告、寻求、转离），描述了真心痛悔的各个方面，并且在第10-36章反复出现。"医治他们的地"，包括救治干早和虫害，以及使百姓从被掳之地返回故土（6:38）。对于作者来说，这还包括百姓与上帝恢复正确的关系。参耶25:5,26:3。

textCards.body: 这样，耶和华的殿全然完毕，圣殿完成并不是在建成或奉献的时候，而是在设立了定期的敬拜条例之后。这证明所罗门忠于他所受的托付，而后来有关他作王统治的详细记载（8:17-9:28），表明上帝因他的顺服而赐福予他（见1:12）。

textCards.body: 哈多兰也称为"亚多兰"（见王上12:18注）或"亚多尼兰"（王上4:6,5:14），是所罗门的监工。王国分裂主前931年所罗门死后，他儿子罗波安在示剑继承王位。罗波安拒绝减轻他父亲加在百姓身上的重税，导致北方十个支派反叛并拥立耶罗波安作他们的王。北国现在称为以色列，南国称为犹大。五年后，埃及王示撒（又称舍松克）入侵犹大和以色列，攻占了许多城邑。罗波安为求保住耶路撒冷，把所罗门放在圣殿里的许多珍宝献给示撒求和。

textCards.body: 罗波安在即位之初是一位忠实虔诚的君王，但后来渐渐变得骄傲，只依靠自己的力量而不仰赖上帝。他和百姓离弃耶和华的律法，就等于离弃上帝自己（5节）：人若不顺服上帝启示的旨意，就与他没有实质的关系。《新约》也明确地指出这一点，耶稣说，遵守他的命令就是爱他（约14:21）。

textCards.body: 得罪（《和修》"背叛"）希伯来原文ma'al，是作者使用的一个关键词，见代上2:3-8注。犹大国民背道，随即埃及人人侵，作者显然将埃及的人侵视为上帝对罪的惩罚。然而.《历代志》所记载的窘迫或苦难并非都可以这样理解，如20:1-12、32:1，犹大王行事敬虔，但仍然受到外族人侵：13:8也一样。第五年，即主前925年（见王上14:25-26注）。示撒即舍松克一世，于主前945-主前924年统治埃及。他与犹大和以色列的战事铭刻在卡纳克

textCards.body: 作者对罗波安的整体评价是负面的：王上14:22（见该处注释）指责百姓"行恶"，而《历代志》却指出行恶者是罗波安，并补充说：他不立定心意寻求耶和华（参12:15-16这两节基本上取材于王上14:29-31，但经文示玛雅和易多的史记也是作者的资料来源（见王上14:19注）。《历代志》中包含了一些《列王纪》中没有的材这些材料大都来自上述资料。

textCards.body: 从第8节亚比雅的话来判断，可能是耶罗波安挑起结6:4,6了这场战争，寻求用武力重新统一整个王国，罗波安也曾尝试这样做（11:1-4）。关于军队的人数，见代上12:23-37注。无论我们怎样理解那些数字，犹大军队的人数始终只有以色列军队的一半。

textCards.body: 洗玛脸山可能位于便雅悯支派的北部边界，在南国与北国的交界处，见书18:22。亚比雅的讲话是《历代志》记载的几次国王讲话中的一次，作者以此来表达他所关切的事—在这次讲话中，他要谴责北国背道和持续反叛。

textCards.body: 古实王谢拉（《和修》"古实人谢拉"），古实即现代的苏丹（见12:3.16:8）。这个人没有出现在其他地方，可能是舍松克一世（12:2）之子，法老奥索尔康一世手下的一位将领。一百万直译是"一千个干"，代表一个很大的数字，也可以理解为"一千个单位"（见代上12:23-37注）。这支军队比亚撒率领的军队多出将近一倍（14:8）。玛利沙是罗波安的一座坚固城，位于犹大的西南边境（11:8）。

textCards.body: 太后是王的顾问，也是王子的老师，在宫廷中扮演着重要的角色。汲沦溪或称"汲沦谷"'，就在耶路撒冷城外，是弃置拜偶像之物的垃圾场（29:16,30:14）。考古人员在靠近现代希伯仑的科姆遗址发现了一块石碑，上面刻着："耶和华和他的亚舍拉赐福与乌利亚胡；耶和华拯救他脱离了仇敌！"碑文的年代是主前8世纪的下半叶，反映在犹大国内，耶和华真正的仆人与把异教混杂到信仰中的人及拜偶像者之间，一直处于斗争状态。

太后是王的顾问，也是王子的老师，在宫廷中扮演着重要的角色。汲沦溪或称"汲沦谷"'，就在耶路撒冷城外，是弃置拜偶像之物的垃圾场（29:16,30:14）。考古人员在靠近现代希伯仑的科姆遗址发现了一块石碑，上面刻着："耶和华和他的亚舍拉赐福与乌利亚胡；耶和华拯救他脱离了仇敌！"碑文的年代是主前8世纪的下半叶，反映在犹大国内，耶和华真正的仆人与把异教混杂到信仰中的人及拜偶像者之间，一直处于斗争状态。

textCards.body: 亚撒三十六年约为主前876或主前875年，若是这样，经文就出现一个问题，因为巴沙这时已经去世10年了（见王上15:33，16:8：王上15:17注）。一个可能的解释是：这里的经文抄写有误。希伯来文字母起初用于表示数字，在这里（以及15:19），文士可能混淆了两个非常相似的希伯来文字母，就是代表10的、或yod和代表30的或lamedh；在早期的手抄希伯来经卷中，这两个字母比现代印刷体更加相似。若是这样，原本可能是说这事发生在"亚撒作王第十六年"，即主前896或主前895年。拉玛位于耶路撒冷以北约8公里处，控制着进出耶城的主要通道。

textCards.body: 亚撒因此恼恨先见，按照《旧约》的记载，亚撒是第一个迫害先知的君王（见18:26.24:21,25:1636:16）。将他囚在监里，使人联想到耶利米所遭受的通（耶20:2）。16:11-12犹大和以色列诸王记，见12:15，13:22：以及王上14:19注。作者没有明确说明，亚撒的脚病是不是上帝惩罚他缺乏信心和虐待哈拿尼，但经文可能有这个暗示。

textCards.body: 基列的拉末位于加利利海的东南方，可能是特拉米思，靠近现代约旦的赖姆（见申4:43）。叙利亚人在便哈达统治时期（约主前860-主前843年）夺取了这座城。约沙法的话表明他愿意遵守和亚哈签订的条约。

textCards.body: 耶和华却因自己与大卫所立的约（见代上17:14），这是作者在他的资料来源上添加的评注。永远赐灯光与大卫和他的子孙，这个比喻意指：即使在最黑暗的时候，上帝仍然存留大卫家，圣殿内的灯台常常点着也许正要表明这个应许（13:11）。后文记载，大卫的家族由于谋杀和战争而近乎断绝（21:4.17，22:10），危如累卵。尽管局势不容乐观，但王国仍有上帝恩典的保守，可是约兰仍要承受他自己作恶的惩罚（21:10-20；参王下8:19注，8:20-22注）。

textCards.body: 会众是由百姓领袖组成的代表团体。聪明的君王每次遇到大事时，都会寻求这个团体的建议和支持（见代上13:2,29:1：代下30:2）。与王立约，这份条约可能关乎约阿施执政的安排，包括由耶何耶大摄政，直到约阿施成年（见24:4）。王的儿子必当作王，正如耶和华指着大卫孙所应许的话，作者的补充，肯定了这次政变是要成就帝保守大卫血统的应许（参代上17:14；代下6:10，21:7）。
textCards.searchText:

textCards.body: 重修（直译：更新），在此之前，圣殿的物品曾被亚他利雅和她的儿子们掠夺（7节），圣殿可能一直疏于管24:5-6、9修理你们上帝的殿，见王下12:4-5注。《列王纪》提到修理圣殿的经费有三个来源（参王下12:4），但《历代志》只提到摩西为了建造和维护会幕而征收人头税（见出30:16）。摩西会幕是圣殿的预表，两者之间的一致性和连续性是《历代志》作者特有的主题之一（见3-4章的注释）。24:5-6说祭司和利未人没有办好这件事，这样的批评实属罕见。

textCards.body: 乌西雅的统治包含两段共同执政的时期：一段是和父亲亚玛谢共同执政（主前796-主前767年），另一段是和儿子约坦共同执政（主前750-主前733年）。先知以赛亚在乌西雅作王期间开始侍奉（赛1:1,6:1）。

textCards.body: 撒迦利亚是乌西雅一名敬虔的谋士，就像耶何耶大作约阿施的谋士那样（24:2）。这位撤迦利亚的其他事迹不详。他不是24:20-21所记述的撒迎利亚，也不是《撒迎利亚书》的作者，因为后者在主前520年才开始侍奉。

textCards.body: 欣嫩子谷，见33:6：耶7:31。利20:1-5用最严厉的话谴责迎南人献儿女为祭的行为。28:5-7见王下16:5-7；赛7:1-6。作者指出，亚兰和以色列的进攻是上帝的审判，因为亚哈斯和百姓离弃了耶和华。虽然联军没能攻取耶路撒冷，但他们显然已经占领城郊；犹大国民被掳到大马士革，可以说是预演将来的被掳（36:20）。关于对数字的理解，见代上12:23-37注。

textCards.body: 食物的收取从三月（5-6月，七七节或五旬节）的五谷收割开始，一直持续到七月（9-10月，收藏节或住棚节）果子和葡萄的收成。在这些节期和逾越节期间，以色列所有男丁都要上到圣殿守节（出23:16-17）。

textCards.body: 又筑一城（《和修》"筑另一片城墙》，1970年，在对耶路撒冷旧城的犹太人区进行的发掘中，考古学家纳曼阿维加得发现了一堵主前8世纪-主前7世纪建成的围墙，厚6.4米。这城墙可能就是希西家修筑用来抵御亚述军侵略的。扩建这片城墙也是为了解决北国难民涌人而导致的人口增长问题。米罗（"米罗"是音译，指土堆的防御工事），见撒下5:9注。

textCards.body: 犹大言语（《和修》"犹大话"是希伯来语：当时，亚兰语是近东地区的国际通用语言（见王下18:26-35）。在耶路撒冷的历史中，西山是首个有城墙围绕保护的地方。欣嫩子谷御花园一座巨大的堤坝封闭中央山谷的入口（后来犹太历史学家约瑟夫称中央山谷为泰罗边谷）。这个坝同时成为御花园的南边城墙。这城墙。在耶路撒冷旧城的犹太人区考古发掘工作中，发现了这堵墙的延伸段（长达64米）。以法莲门？角门？中央山谷粪厂门西罗亚池为西山的居民提供水源。

textCards.body: 希西家王和…先知以赛亚，作者省略了希西家祷告的细节，以及以赛亚关于得拯救的预言（见王下19:15-34），强调上帝应许聆听百姓在急难中的祷告（参6:24-25）。向天呼求，参王下19:1-2注。希西家加强耶路撒冷的防卫约主前702年先知预言亚述要侵袭犹大，因此犹大王希西家加强耶路撒冷的防卫，修葺破损的城墙，改造水道，把基训泉的水引入城中。他给军队配备武器和盾牌，任命将领管理百姓。考古证据显示，希西家还建造新城墙环绕城西边的山，并在所罗门圣殿周围建造圣殿山。1000 2000英尺上池？◎ 500米哈米亚楼7哈楠业楼2-9D圣殿 圣殿山宽墙谷门一，品基训泉大卫城米罗？希西家水道下池（西罗亚池）一～御花园？欣嫩子谷『隐罗结32:21见王下19:35-37。满面含羞地，这是本书作者补充的，突出微慢的亚述王和上帝对抗的结果（见诗34:4-7，35:4-5）。虽然西拿基立的军队很快就撤退了，然而直到主前681年，即20年之后，他才被杀（见王下19:35-36注，19:37注）。

textCards.body: 耶和华警戒玛拿西和他的百姓，暗指王下21:10，并且用来总结王下21:11-15有关审判的预言。他们却是不听是根据王下21:9a。玛拿西和百姓拒绝听从上帝的话，这种态度预示了王国末期的光景（36:15-16）。

textCards.body: 玛拿西被囚禁在巴比伦，这件事只出现在《圣经》。但这段记载经常与以下历史事件相关联：主前652-主前648年，统治巴比伦的沙马士沈乌金可能因为早前被迫臣服于弟弟，就是统治亚述的霸主亚述巴尼帕，所以大规模地反抗弟弟的统治。内战持续了五年，最终亚述巴尼帕攻取了巴比伦，他随即把注意力转向帝国的西部及其附庸国，包括犹大国。玛拿西可能参与叛变，或至少被怀疑支持这场叛变。

textCards.body: 玛拿西建筑城墻和设立军长，可能是因为沙马士沈乌金的叛变被镇压下来后，亚述需要在西南部扶持一个缓冲国，以对抗埃及。有能力大兴土木和拥有强大军队，是忠心的王蒙福的典型标记（见11:5-12，14:6-8，17:12-19，26:9-15）。

textCards.body: 律法书通常被认为是《申命记》卷轴或其中一部分。在修缮圣殿的过程中发现律法书，成为继续改革的重要动力（34:29-33；参王下22:8注），因为这件事本身就是上帝对百姓忠心侍奉的奖赏。
